flashcards.Back: The physical network of routers, switches, and cabling that provides basic Layer 3 IP connectivity between all devices in a software-defined architecture.
A logical network that runs on top of the underlay, creating virtual links between endpoints. it uses encapsulation to separate user traffic from the physical topology.
The unified system formed by the combination of the underlay and the overlay. it provides automated connectivity and policy enforcement for all endpoints.
Virtual Extensible LAN. The standard encapsulation protocol used in Cisco's SD-Access overlay to transport Layer 2 frames over a Layer 3 underlay.
Locator/ID Separation Protocol. The technology used in the SDA control plane to track the location of endpoints independently of their IP addresses.
An SDA switch that connects directly to endpoints (like PCs and APs). it handles the entry and exit of traffic to the fabric.
An SDA device that connects the automated fabric to external networks, such as the internet, a shared data center, or traditional legacy networks.
The SDA device that maintains the LISP mapping database, tracking where every user and device is currently located within the network.
A metadata tag used in SDA to define security and QoS policies based on a user's role rather than their IP address or VLAN.
The ability of an administrator to define business goals in DNA Center, which are then automatically translated into fabric-wide configurations.
The SDN principle where the 'brains' of the network (decision making) are decoupled from the hardware that actually moves packets (data plane).
A REST-based interface on an SDN controller used by external applications and scripts to automate network-wide management tasks.
The communication protocol used by a controller to manage individual switches and routers. examples include NETCONF, RESTCONF, and OpenFlow.
A modern southbound protocol that uses XML data encoding and SSH transport to program device configurations reliably.
An HTTP-based southbound protocol that provides a RESTful interface for accessing and manipulating device configurations.
A legacy SDN protocol that allows a controller to directly program the flow tables of network switches.
The ability to run multiple independent logical networks (virtual networks) across a single physical fabric underlay.
An SDA feature where the default gateway for a VLAN exists on every fabric edge switch simultaneously, improving performance for mobile users.
In LISP, the IP address assigned to a user device that stays the same even if the user moves to a different physical location.
In LISP, the IP address of the fabric switch that a specific user device is currently connected to.
The process of wrapping a user packet inside a new header (like VXLAN) to transport it across the underlay network.
Cisco's enterprise campus SDN solution that uses DNA Center to automate network provisioning and security segmentation.
The physical hardware in a switch that performs high-speed packet forwarding based on the rules provided by the SDN controller.
The centralized dashboard in DNA Center where administrators design, monitor, and troubleshoot the entire network fabric.
The ability of DNA Center to automatically configure all switches in an SDA fabric according to a predefined design template.
The enforcement of security rules in a fabric based on user identity and group membership rather than fixed IP subnets.
The high-level perspective provided by an SDN controller, allowing it to see the entire network topology and traffic flows.
The automated coordination of multiple network components and services to deliver a complete end-to-end application workflow.
A standardized data modeling language used by NETCONF and RESTCONF to define the structure of network configurations.
The property of software-defined architectures that allows them to support massive growth by adding nodes without increasing management complexity.
